title: Ambient basic geometry
summary: Introduces the formal ambient bundle, its dilation and boundary inclusion, and the data used to represent a straight-form ambient metric.

title: Ambient densities
summary: Defines ambient densities as homogeneous functions under positive dilation and restricts them to conformal densities on the hypersurface rho = 0.

title: Ambient operator calculus
summary: Packages a formal calculus of Q, the Euler operator X, and the ambient Laplacian, then proves its consequences under an explicit interface of algebraic identities.

title: Tangential ambient operators
summary: Formalizes equality modulo Q and weighted tangentiality, packages a formal GJMS candidate, and records that critical tangentiality remains planned here.

title: FG
summary: Aggregate entry point for the Fefferman–Graham straight ambient normal-form component calculus.

title: FG calculus bridge
summary: Transfers a certified FG normal-form Laplacian model into the ambient Calculus and AlgebraicIdentities interfaces.

title: Normal-form Christoffel components
summary: Stores the rho derivative and base Christoffel data, then defines the selected raised-index ambient components used later.

title: FG commutator identities
summary: Exposes the Q–X–ambient-Laplacian identities contained in NormalLaplacianModel under names used by tangentiality.

title: Finite formal metric jets
summary: Defines finite component-level rho-jets and packages the first FG condition g^(1) = 2P as an explicit hypothesis.

title: FG normal-form indices
summary: Introduces the three index directions and coordinate triple used in FG normal-form component calculations.

title: Inverse normal-form metric
summary: Defines the expected inverse block components and proves the base-block left-inverse identity from an explicit hypothesis.

title: FG defining function and Laplacian model
summary: Defines Q = 2 rho t^2 and packages the Euler operator and ambient Laplacian with the laws required downstream.

title: FG normal form
summary: Packages straight ambient data and defines the FG normal-form component matrix in the t, base, and rho directions.

title: Ambient obstruction scaffold
summary: Stores even-dimensional obstruction-order data, a symmetric component tensor, and an abstract vanishing proposition.

title: Finite FG recursion scaffold
summary: Packages coefficients declared to agree with a finite metric jet; no recursive solver is implemented yet.

title: FG Ricci equations in normal form
summary: Defines symbolic normal-form Ricci expressions and exposes them through an explicit component certificate.

title: PE
summary: Aggregate entry point for Poincaré–Einstein geometry, the ambient bridge, invariants, renormalization, and applications.

title: Ambient–PE bridge
summary: Records the coordinate change ρ = −r²/2, s = rt, the relation hᵣ = g(−r²/2), and the ambient cone decomposition.

title: PE applications
summary: Aggregate import for the Case–Khaitan–Lin–Tyrrell–Yuan finite-part identity and its Pfaffian specialization.

title: CaseKhaitanLinTyrrellYuan
summary: Assembles the formal finite-part identity corresponding to Theorem 1.4 from explicit evaluation, recursion, divergence, and renormalization interfaces.

title: PfaffianApplication
summary: Packages the Pfaffian-like specialization and formal Theorem 1.1 assembly.

title: PE foundations
summary: Introduces the abstract bulk-boundary data and the formal metric-expression language used by the Poincaré–Einstein layer.

title: Defining functions
summary: Encodes boundary defining functions, the compactification ḡ = r²g₊, and the geodesic normalization through explicit certificate fields.

title: Einstein equation and parity
summary: Packages the equation Ric(g₊) = −ng₊ together with a formal hᵣ-expansion whose odd coefficients below order n vanish.

title: PE invariants
summary: Aggregate import for the symbolic invariant, ambient-extension, Laplacian-recursion, divergence, and Pfaffian modules.

title: LaplacianRecursion
summary: Formalizes the iterative ambient-Laplacian recursion corresponding to Proposition 3.7 in the PE renormalized-integral reference.

title: NaturalDivergence
summary: Packages natural divergences and the cone-rule interface used to convert ambient identities into PE divergence statements.

title: Pfaffian
summary: Defines Pfaffian-like symbolic invariants and the P_l_n family used in the Gauss–Bonnet-type application.

title: RiemannianInvariant
summary: Defines symbolic scalar/tensor Riemannian invariants with rank, conformal weight, tensor weight, and parity bookkeeping.

title: Straightenable
summary: Defines invariants that admit evaluation through a chosen ambient model and proves closure operations at the interface level.

title: Metric components
summary: Expands the Poincaré normal form into radial–tangential blocks and proves that the displayed contravariant block is a left inverse when hⁱʲ is a left inverse of hᵢⱼ and r ≠ 0.

title: Poincaré normal form
summary: Packages the family hᵣ and the identity g₊ = r⁻²(dr² + hᵣ), with the collar and boundary identifications supplied as interface data.

title: PE renormalization
summary: Aggregate import for Laurent/log expansions, finite parts, cutoff functionals, renormalized volume, curvature integrals, and divergence vanishing.

title: CurvatureIntegral
summary: Specializes the finite-part framework to renormalized integrals of curvature invariants.

title: CutoffFunctional
summary: Packages cutoff-dependent quantities whose asymptotic expansion defines a renormalized value.

title: DivergenceVanishing
summary: States the weighted divergence-vanishing interface corresponding to the parity input used in Lemma 4.1.

title: FinitePart
summary: Defines the finite part as the constant Laurent coefficient and the log anomaly as the logarithmic coefficient.

title: LaurentLogExpansion
summary: Models a finite Laurent/log expansion, parity conditions, coefficient support, and logarithmic terms.

title: Volume
summary: Specializes cutoff functionals to renormalized PE volume and its anomaly.

title: Basic
summary: Models a conformal structure as a metric bundle with positive rescaling, together with scales and the comparison of representatives.

title: Density
summary: Defines conformal densities through equivariance under positive metric rescaling and relates equivariant and scale-dependent presentations.

title: Ambient Laplacian construction
summary: Shows that Δ̃ᵏ has the GJMS weight shift under AlgebraicIdentities, then packages it as a weighted tangential and abstract GJMS operator once tangentiality is supplied.

title: GJMS weights and abstract operators
summary: Defines the critical weights wₖ = k − n/2 and wₖ′ = −k − n/2, then packages weighted tangential operators and their low-order family notation.

title: Critical-weight tangentiality
summary: Proves algebraically that Δ̃ᵏ preserves boundary values at weight k − n/2, then assembles the canonical abstract GJMS operator family.

title: Juhl combinatorics module aggregate
summary: Collects the formal jet recursion, low-order syntax checks, formal statement, abstract OR bridge, and curved-OR assembly under one import path.

title: Formal curved OR Juhl assembly
summary: Composes a stored formal Juhl statement with a stored formal Juhl–OR bridge and instantiates the construction at orders one and two using only identity bridges.

title: Formal jet equations
summary: Defines a syntax tree for the symbols in the Juhl obstruction recursion and assembles the formal coefficient equation Lₘ from its highest-jet, trace-derivative, Laplacian-variation, and zeroth-order trace terms.

title: Formal Juhl statement scaffold
summary: Defines normalized syntax equality and data structures for a finite formal Juhl right-hand side, then packages machine-checked order-one and raw order-two identities.

title: Low-order formal syntax checks
summary: Implements a limited syntax normalizer and machine-checks that the recursively generated formal expressions at orders one and two match explicitly written raw P₂ and P₄ syntax trees.

title: Formal Juhl–OR bridge
summary: Introduces abstract data asserting coefficient and normalized-expression agreement between a formal Juhl side and a formal OR side; no conversion from actual OR operators is implemented.

title: Formal obstruction recursion
summary: Recursively solves the formal equations Lₛ=0 for normal jets Fₛ₊₁ and defines a normalized formal obstruction expression P₂ℓ, without interpreting it as a geometric differential operator.

title: Integer compositions for Juhl formulas
summary: Defines ordered compositions I=(I₁,…,Iᵣ) of N with positive parts and provides the empty, singleton, and two-part constructors.

title: Juhl coefficient-system scaffold
summary: Defines abstract direct and inverse coefficient families and basic composition statistics, but the advertised closed coefficients m_I and n_I are currently placeholders equal to one.

title: Composition of abstract GJMS operators
summary: Defines ordinary unary-operator composition and associates an ordered composition I with the formal composite P₂I₁∘⋯∘P₂Iᵣ of an abstract GJMS family.

title: Canonical GJMS compositions
summary: Specializes abstract Juhl compositions to the canonical ambient GJMS family and verifies that the singleton composition is the ambient Laplacian power Δ̃ᴺ under the algebraic-identities interface.

title: Multi-input operator foundations
summary: Defines rank-r tuples of ambient functions, rank-r operators, unary adapters, slot replacement, and slotwise unary mapping.

title: Bidifferential specialization
summary: Specializes multi-input notation to two slots, with weights (w₁,w₂), paired inputs (f,g), and expected output weight w₁+w₂−2N.

title: Boundary equivalence for multi-inputs
summary: Lifts unary equality modulo Q and its weighted refinement pointwise to every slot of a rank-r ambient input tuple.

title: Multiweight homogeneity
summary: Assigns one ambient homogeneity to each input slot, sums the input weights, and defines the expected output weight after total Laplacian degree N.

title: Laplacian monomial words
summary: Encodes ambient-Laplacian monomials of the form Δ̃ᵃ(∏ᵢ Δ̃ᵇⁱFᵢ), proves their output homogeneity from explicit algebraic and product-homogeneity interfaces, and specializes the syntax to two and three inputs.

title: Finite sums of ambient operators
summary: Defines finite real linear combinations of rank-r ambient operators and proves that a finite sum of operators tangential at the same input weights remains tangential.

title: Algebra of multi-operators
summary: Defines pointwise zero, addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and unary pre- and post-composition for rank-r ambient operators.

title: Ovsienko–Redou module aggregate
summary: Provides a single import path for the OR index, coefficient, operator-data, defect-cancellation, and closed-operator layers.

title: Regular closed OR operator
summary: Instantiates OR operator data with the regular closed rational coefficients and proves tangentiality conditional on a compatible defect expansion and rational cancellation proof.

title: Regular closed OR coefficients
summary: Defines the rational/Pochhammer form of the regular Ovsienko–Redou coefficient and packages it as a coefficient system, while leaving its global defect-cancellation recurrence separate.

title: Closed-form recurrence bridge
summary: Records the conditional bridge from a supplied rational defect-cancellation proof to the closed-form recurrence predicate and imports the independently proved two local Pochhammer recurrences.

title: Rational coefficient combinatorics
summary: Defines factorial, binomial, trinomial, rising-factorial, and falling-factorial expressions over the rationals and proves their zero, successor, and predecessor product identities.

title: Regular OR coefficient parameters
summary: Introduces the half-dimension shift and the three rational Pochhammer parameters used in the regular Ovsienko–Redou coefficient formula, with one algebraic rewrite.

title: Local OR coefficient recurrences
summary: Proves the two adjacent-index recurrence identities for the regular Pochhammer factor by moving one unit of Laplacian degree from the output exponent to either input exponent.

title: OR coefficient and cancellation predicates
summary: Defines rational coefficient systems and expresses the intended OR recurrence as vanishing of every finite obstruction-coefficient sum.

title: OR defect cancellation and tangentiality
summary: Expresses the boundary defect of an OR-indexed operator in a finite obstruction basis, converts rational cancellation to real cancellation, and proves conditional bidifferential tangentiality.

title: OR degree indices and adjacent moves
summary: Encodes triples (a,b,c) with a+b+c=N for the words Δ̃ᵃ((Δ̃ᵇf)(Δ̃ᶜg)), constructs their finite support, and defines degree-preserving adjacent moves.

title: OR operators from rational coefficients
summary: Turns a rational degree-N OR coefficient system into a concrete finite ambient bidifferential sum and proves its common output weight under product and finite-sum homogeneity interfaces.

title: Abstract Ovsienko–Redou construction
summary: Specializes fixed-degree poly-Laplacian combinations to two inputs and shows how weight mapping plus defect cancellation produce a weighted bidifferential tangential operator.

title: Fixed-degree poly-Laplacian combinations
summary: Packages finite sums of rank-r Laplacian words with a common total degree, proves their common output weight under homogeneity interfaces, and derives tangentiality from an explicit defect expansion and coefficient cancellation.

title: Multiweight tangential operators
summary: Defines multi-input tangentiality and weight mapping, packages both properties, and proves closure of tangentiality under sums and scalar multiples.

title: Tree-operator module aggregate
summary: Collects the left-comb tree-word, recurrence, defect-cancellation, and first chain-complex modules under one import path.

title: First tree-recurrence complex
summary: Records stars-and-bars dimensions, a proposed Euler characteristic, the first recurrence differential, and a placeholder package for a future generic exactness proof.

title: Tree recurrence and tangentiality interface
summary: Builds finite sums of left-comb words and proves their tangentiality once a RecurrenceImpliesTangentiality principle is supplied together with a recurrent coefficient system.

title: Left-comb Laplacian words
summary: Defines rank-general nested Laplacian monomials on a left-comb binary tree, their degree indices, vertex updates, multinomial coefficients, and recursive evaluation.

title: Path-wise tree recurrence
summary: Defines the left-comb paths, subtrees, vertex defect coefficients, and the first recurrence differential governing the intended slot-wise Q-commutator cancellation.
